seo.title: Docker & Containers tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Docker & Containers tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of tools and resources for containerization, focusing on image optimization, security, and local development efficiency.

entries.name: Hadolint
entries.description: A smarter Dockerfile linter that helps you build best-practice Docker images by parsing the Dockerfile into an AST and performing static analysis.
pros: Validates inline bash scripts within Dockerfiles
Integrates directly into VS Code and CI pipelines
cons: Requires Haskell environment if building from source

entries.name: Dive
entries.description: A tool for exploring a docker image, layer contents, and discovering ways to shrink the size of your Docker/OCI image.
pros: Shows exactly what changed in each layer
Integrates into CI to fail builds if efficiency is too low
cons: CLI-only interface may be difficult for complex layer trees

entries.name: Trivy
entries.description: A comprehensive security scanner for vulnerabilities in container images, file systems, and Git repositories.
pros: Detects OS package vulnerabilities and language-specific dependencies
cons: Database updates can be large and slow on first run

entries.name: Traefik Proxy
entries.description: An edge router that automatically discovers container configurations to route traffic to backend services.
cons: Steep learning curve for complex middleware configurations

entries.name: Portainer
entries.description: A centralized service delivery platform for container management with a user-friendly GUI.
pros: Simplifies container management for non-CLI users
cons: Adds additional overhead to the host system

entries.name: Podman
entries.description: A daemonless container engine for developing, managing, and running OCI Containers on Linux systems.
pros: Daemonless architecture improves security and stability
Compatible with Docker CLI commands via alias
cons: Compatibility issues with some Docker-specific volumes
Limited support on Windows/macOS compared to Docker Desktop

entries.name: Coolify
entries.description: An open-source and self-hostable alternative to Heroku / Netlify for managing Docker-based deployments.
cons: Less mature than enterprise PaaS solutions

entries.name: BuildKit
entries.description: The next-generation build toolkit for Docker that enables parallel stage execution and cache exports.
pros: Significantly faster build times through concurrency
Support for secrets without baking them into layers
cons: Requires modern Docker versions and DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 flag

entries.name: Lazydocker
entries.description: A simple terminal UI for both docker and docker-compose, written in Go with gocui.
pros: Keyboard-driven interface for fast container management
Visualizes CPU and memory usage in real-time
One-key cleaning of unused images and volumes

entries.name: Watchtower
entries.description: A process for automating Docker container base image updates.
pros: Automatically pulls new images and restarts containers
cons: Risk of breaking production if semver is not strictly followed

entries.name: Docker Slim
entries.description: Minifies Docker images by analyzing application behavior to remove unnecessary files.
pros: Reduces image size by up to 30x
Improves security by reducing attack surface
cons: Analysis phase can be slow for large applications
May remove files required for edge-case runtime scenarios

entries.name: Kaniko
entries.description: A tool to build container images from a Dockerfile inside a container or Kubernetes cluster without a Docker daemon.
cons: Build performance can be slower than BuildKit
Caching requires external storage like GCS or S3
